meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn how to architect SaaS pricing models that drive revenue. A professional case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The root of 'no project ideas' is often a lack of revenue-focused constraints. By engineering your SaaS pricing model first, you create a commercial framework that forces product-market fit. Use Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to iterate on high-conversion payment logic that validates your business model immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. Most founders lose months agonizing over 'the next big idea,' paralyzed by the lack of a north star. The truth? Ideas are cheap; business models are rare. If you don't know what to build, start by defining what people will pay for.

By flipping the script and architecting your pricing strategy before writing a line of feature code, you pivot from 'building a tool' to 'scaling a business.' This case study demonstrates how to use modern AI agents to reverse-engineer a profitable SaaS architecture, turning the existential dread of a blank project into a validated, revenue-generating engine.

steps.title: Define the Value Metric
steps.description: Identify a pricing metric that scales with customer usage (e.g., API calls, seats, storage) to ensure your revenue grows alongside your users' success.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following problem space [INSERT NICHE] and propose three SaaS pricing models based on different value metrics. Evaluate each for ease of implementation and ARR potential.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the business viability of a usage-based vs flat-fee pricing model for a [NICHE] SaaS product.'

steps.title: Architect the Subscription Schema
steps.description: Create a robust database schema that supports tiered pricing, allowing you to move users between plans without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS platform with three tiers (Basic, Pro, Enterprise). Include fields for subscription status, usage limits, and billing cycle.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add schema/subscription_model.sql --context 'Ensure compliance with Stripe billing structures.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration Logic
steps.description: Build the backend controller to handle webhook events, ensuring your application state perfectly mirrors the payment gateway status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Node.js controller for handling Stripe webhooks. Focus on 'customer.subscription.updated' and 'invoice.payment_failed' events to trigger internal access updates.

steps.title: Dynamic Feature Flagging
steps.description: Connect your pricing tiers to feature access. This keeps the codebase clean and allows for instant 'Pro' upgrades via the dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware utility that checks the user's subscription tier against requested features. Use a map-based configuration to define tier permissions.

steps.title: Conversion-Optimized Pricing UI
steps.description: Build a high-converting pricing table that highlights the 'Pro' plan as the logical choice for professional users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React pricing component using Tailwind CSS. Include a toggle for monthly vs. annual billing with a 20% discount highlighted for annual.

pro_tips: Always start with an Annual-first pricing strategy to improve cash flow and reduce churn metrics early.
Use 'Price Anchoring' by adding an Enterprise tier that is 3x more expensive than your Pro tier to make the middle option look like a deal.
Automate your dunning process (failed payment handling) immediately; it can recover 15-20% of lost ARR.
Don't build your own billing engine; leverage Stripe's Customer Portal to let users manage their own upgrades and cancellations.
Conduct 'Pricing Discovery' by offering a pre-sale to 5 people before writing the code; if they won't pay, don't build it.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing page to a staging environment, connect it to your Stripe Test Mode account, and perform a full end-to-end checkout flow test.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing what to build and start building what sells. By engineering your pricing model first, you remove the guesswork and build a business that scales. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
